next_steps: Perform immunohistochemical staining for BRAF V600E mutation on bone biopsy tissue
Consult hematology/oncology for bone marrow aspirate and biopsy with flow cytometry and cytogenetics
Obtain serum and urine protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and free light chain assay
Check serum levels of IL-6 and soluble IL-2 receptor
Initiate systemic treatment with interferon-alpha or a BRAF inhibitor if mutation is confirmed
Coordinate skeletal survey and orthopedic consultation for stabilization of long bone lesions
Perform immunohistochemical staining on bone biopsy tissue for CD1a, CD68, and Factor XIIIa
Test for BRAF V600E mutation on bone tissue biopsy
Measure serum vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) levels
Perform serum and urine protein electrophoresis with immunofixation and free light chain assay
Conduct a formal neurological examination with nerve conduction studies and electromyography to rule out polyneuropathy
Perform a specialized histopathology review focusing on histiocytic markers vs. plasma cell clonality
Coordinate orthopedic evaluation for prophylactic stabilization of long-bone lesions
